description: Tracks public support for the Kenya Kwanza / ODM 'Broad-Based Government' arrangement over time, and short, sourced notes on the coalition realignment context following Raila Odinga's death. This is a structural/organizational signal, not a candidate-preference number — it is about whether the current governing coalition holds together, which materially changes the entire 2027 candidate and alliance map.

support_series.notes: This figure is cited retrospectively inside the May 2026 release as a comparison point; the original November 2025 release was not independently located, so this single number should be treated as secondary-sourced. Opposition percentage for this wave was not reported.

support_series.source_title: Ruto support weakening but fragmented opposition could hand him 2027 lifeline: poll
support_series.notes: Primary wave for this reading, from the same TIFA release as the May 2026 candidate poll and the alliance-preference figures.

context_notes.note: The May 2026 TIFA release frames the drop in Broad-Based Government support against 'jostling within the Broad-Based Government coalition arrangement... amid push and pull over zoning and a 2027 coalition framework following the death of Raila Odinga', with ODM figures reportedly in active talks about the coalition's future.

known_gaps: This is two data points, not a real trend line, and the November 2025 figure is secondary-sourced. This tracker exists to be extended honestly as more waves are found or published — not to be treated as settled. No numeric 'coalition realignment index' is computed here because there isn't enough real data yet to justify one; fabricating one would repeat exactly the mistake this project has spent this whole build correcting for.
